entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Singapore Peppol eInvoices (InvoiceNow) - Part 2
entry.content:
Hi @Chanchana,

I've completed all the config mentioned in the SAP Help except the logical ports which I don't have authorizations as a functional. But still in the EDOC_COCKPIT, the billing document is unchecked with "IS relevant" flag. Because of this I couldn't able to generate the eDOCUMENT.

Please suggest what I'm missing.

--

Thank you & Regards

entry.title: Re: Benefits of Executing Stored Procedures via API in SAP SuccessFactors Incentive Management
entry.content: Excellent article which can reduce raising support tickets for deployments to production. Great thinking and need like this many more features to have a self service instead of dependency on someone.

entry.title: I'm not able to find the cost element box
Re: BAdI to change PMP0 value of PO Item
entry.content:
Hi,

You can try BAdI PRCG_DOC_CONDITION_AMOUNT and PRCG_DOC_ITM_CNDN_BASE_VALUE to see if these BAdIs can meet your requirement.

Best Regards,

Zhehui

entry.title: Implement Request-Reply integration pattern with SAP Advanced Event Mesh in SAP UI5 and SAP CPI
entry.content:
Introduction

Synchronous systems often have a high probability of encountering errors due to the interdependency that exists between the participant systems. These types of interactions can be made resilient and error-proof by implementing the request-reply integration pattern. In this video, we will demonstrate how we can implement this pattern in SAPUI5 and SAP CPI with SAP Advanced Event Mesh.

 

Demonstration

https://youtu.be/nNr9MeIKuAc?si=EolluNMGQ5A3bhW6 

GitHub Link for demo: https://github.com/deepankar-bhowmick/sap-aem-weather-demo.git

entry.title: Re: How to change the original language of a development...
entry.content:
Hi,

Not sure if this function has been developed.

But I see another question. You can refer it and see whether it helps. Solved: ADT logon language - SAP Community

Best Regards,

Zhehui

entry.title: Re: Unloading point (PP delivery)
entry.content:
You can check unloading points in SO (VA03), "Uploading point" are typically defined in the customer master record for the ship-to party.

Reference link: Unloading Points | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Query to show "Production Order" and related "Issue f...
entry.content: Hi Azacc, try to join to OIGN first from OWOR because the bin location is tied to receipt from production

entry.title: Re: How to explode BOM using CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2 with only approved ECNs (AENR-RLKEY = '1')?
entry.content:
The latest findings show that there are no relevant parameters in CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2.

The subsequent processing steps are as follows:

  1. Use the output internal table STB from CS_BOM_EXPL_MAT_V2.

  2. If STB-AENNR is not initial,

  3. Then check table AENR where AENR-AENNR = STB-AENNR.

    • If AENR-AEFUN = '1' and AENR-RLKEY <> '1',

    • Then this entry should be filtered out and not printed.

entry.title: Re: Determinación de cuentas automática en MM
entry.content:
Hola, tengo un registro de material no valorado. Imputa en la KBS 81-91. No logro saber de dónde toma las cuentas contables para el asiento. 

Agradecería su ayuda.

Saludos

entry.title: Re: New ABAP application with complex HMI
entry.content:
Please try SAP Screen Personas. You can check how to use this tool in You tube or thru open SAP course.

This tool runs on top of SAP WebGui and it is powerful tool to personalize GUI screens.

entry.title: Re: How to create extension field with reference to prede...
entry.content:
Hi,

Currently its not possible to create Reference KUT Fields in Sales & Service Cloud V2.

https://help.sap.com/docs/CX_NG_SVC/2c87cece32844c91836e535aef8f9642/d3bdfac0c6b141c0bac27408c3ed159f.html?q=Extension+Fields

You can request this as enhancement request in the Influence Forums,

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/campaign/3766

entry.title: Blog Series - Intitial Loads to SAP Advanced Event Mesh
entry.content:
In this blog series we focus on a Push Mechanism with the two Event Enabling Technologies 

  • RAP (RESTful Application Programming Model): coming soon
  • AIFAEM: How to perform Initial Loads & Bulk Init Loads in AIFAEM triggered by an ABAP Program 

as well as a Pull Mechanism by using Odata APIs from SAP BTP Integration Suite: How to Perform Initial Loads from S/4HANA to Advanced Event Mesh with Odata Poll

entry.title: Re: How to Perform Initial Loads from S/4HANA to Advanced Event Mesh with Odata Poll
entry.content:
@David_Davis this depends on the Technology used, which also depends on the Release Level. There are 3 options:

  • RAP recommended for newer releases (>2023)--> https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/rap-business-events-with-advanced-event-mesh-1-connecting-aem-to-s-4hana-on/ba-p/13913581 
  • For older releases these options are available:
    • AIFAEM --> https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/how-to-set-up-enhanced-events-for-advanced-event-mesh-with-application/ba-p/13780160 
    • Event Enablement AddOn (ASAPIO) --> https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/event-driven-architecture-now-available-for-sap-ecc-users/ba-p/13452636

entry.title: Re: New ABAP application with complex HMI
entry.content: HI, maybe you try it vice versa and ask yourself what is my preferred UI tool or language to create this UI. Then you can for example connect your SAP system to a system which support your UI, preferrably BTP Kind regards Robert

entry.title: Re: Taking USOBX tables backup before Su25 update
entry.content:
Hi,

Use Step(3) prior to executing any steps and release the transport. So that for any inconsistencies, you can re-import backup TR.

entry.title: Re: How to divide XML message records (node) wise and pos...
entry.content:

Thank you for your insights, however when i am simulating the iflow with sample data (having multiple project data) it is showing me xml seperated. 

 



But when deployed and tested the whole scenario by post XML record (one by one) on REST API, it is not working (As seen the flow on Trace logs and Integration Flow Model) 

My requirement is : I want to POST project data one by one to a Rest API. 

Regards, 

Prathmesh Sutar

entry.title: SAP Community All Time - 47 to 50 is missing or is it reserved numbers ??
entry.content:
Hi Colleagues, 

I was wondering why 47 to 50 numbers in All time is not showing , is it reserved numbers ?? any BUG for not showing that… There could be members which are not shown up.

entry.title: Re: SAP MDK Web - Debug Sources
entry.content: Thanks for your reply. When debugging my own rules, I inevitably come across the WebClient coding, and since it is only available in a minimized version, it is difficult to understand exactly what is happening. I have a particular problem with date fields: Firstly, I am trying to understand why it is not possible to leave these fields blank initially. The trick of using a switch to determine whether the user wants to use a date or not does not seem to me to be a timely user experience; it makes the screen layout confusing, especially when multiple date fields are displayed. Other modern applications manage this just fine. Secondly, and this is really only a problem in the web environment, I wanted to understand why I can't display my date and time in the local format. Here in Switzerland, we typically use the format DD.MM.YYYY for the date and HH:mm:ss for the time, where we display the hours in 24-hour format (one o'clock in the afternoon is 13:00 and not 1 pm). So I hoped to find a place in the source code where, for example, a configuration setting is queried that controls the format settings.

entry.title: Re: How to load Plan Via flexible upload for 12 months at...
entry.content:
You need to open all periods of the monitor and for upload do not use the task in monitor but the app Flexible Upload of Reported Financial Data, in the file the data must be separated by period.

Birgit

entry.title: South Africa VAT Rate Change
entry.content:
The objective of this blog post is to explain, the impact of South Africa VAT rate changes in SAP Business ByDesign. This short guide will help you to maintain new VAT tax rates in SAP Business ByDesign.

On March 12, 2025, South Africa's Finance Minister Enoch Godongwana announced significant changes to the country's value-added tax (VAT) rate during the national budget speech. These changes are set to impact businesses and consumers alike.

VAT Rate Increase

Starting from May 1, 2025, the VAT rate will increase by 0.5%, bringing it to 15.5%. This will be followed by another 0.5% increase on April 1, 2026, raising the rate to 16%.

Maintaining New VAT Tax Rates in SAP Business ByDesign
To ensure compliance with the new VAT rates, users of SAP Business ByDesign need to implement the following settings:
1. Navigate to Business Configuration > Overview > Search for the activity Tax on Goods and Services

Click on Tax Rate Schedule > Add Row
Update VAT rate for the year 2025 valid from Date 01 May 2025 as shown below

Again, click on > Add Row to update VAT rate for the year 2026 valid from Date 01 April 2026 and then Click on Save to reflect the updated VAT rates.

By following these steps, SAP ByDesign users of South Africa can smoothly transition to the new VAT rates and ensure compliance with South Africa's updated tax regulations.

Thanks & Regards

Anusha

SAP Business ByDesign

entry.title: Beyond SAP Cloud - Episode 17: BTP Sub Account for Applications
entry.content:
This video explains the essential services and tools required to build and run applications in SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP). It covers development services like Build Apps and Build Code, process automation, and runtime options such as Cloud Foundry and ABAP. The video also highlights Fiori app deployment, Work Zone for centralized application access, and Task Center for unified inbox management. Additionally, it discusses HANA Cloud, Cloud Integration, connectivity services for external systems, and supporting tools like Cloud Logging and Notification Services to enhance application functionality and troubleshooting. This comprehensive guide helps users understand the key components needed to start building in BTP.

Takeaways

  • Build Apps is a low-code/no-code service for building applications in SAP BTP.
  • Build Code is a pro-code tool for developers to build applications and is the successor of Business Application Studio.
  • Build Apps/Code offers a central lobby to manage various projects and comes with features like storyboard and AI to assist in building applications.
  • Process Automation allows you to design workflows and automations, with the service requiring activation in both test and production systems.
  • Cloud Foundry and ABAP are the most commonly used runtimes in SAP BTP, with Kyma being a less common but emerging option.
  • HTML5 App Repository in Cloud Foundry enables you to deploy Fiori applications without using Cloud Foundry memory, making it free to use.
  • Work Zone serves as a centralized launchpad for all BTP applications, integrating on-premise and cloud applications into one unified interface.
  • Task Center allows the creation of a single inbox to manage tasks from various cloud or on-premise SAP solutions.
  • HANA Cloud requires the creation of an HDI container to connect applications to the database in BTP.
  • Connectivity and Destination Services are essential for integrating external data sources (both on-premise and cloud) into BTP applications.
  • Other services like Cloud Logging and Notification Services help with debugging and automation tasks during application development in BTP.

entry.title: Re: EWM Good Receipt Posting - INV20006 Error
entry.content: Hi @carol_cai, For SFC Complete we are using the Complete Plugin directly from the POD. It is wired that if we pack the HU using Packing List Plugin, it trigger the GR to EWM but it is not when using the packing service from PPD. Do you have any alternate solution on this? I tried EWM GR posting service but that is also throwing error as stock is available. any other options may be? Thanks in advance.

entry.title: Re: add toolbar action on a table to update all rows
entry.content: It's our coding ... non public. YOu can use an action with an input parameter either on entity or on service level.

entry.title: SAP Clean Core: Transforming Legacy Systems for Modern Enterprises
entry.content:
In today's rapidly evolving business landscape, organizations running SAP systems face the challenge of balancing stability with innovation. The concept of "SAP Clean Core" has emerged as a strategic approach to maintain system reliability while enabling agility and digital transformation. This blog explores the fundamentals of SAP Clean Core, its benefits, implementation strategies, and real-world impact.

What is SAP Clean Core?

SAP Clean Core is a modernization philosophy that aims to keep the central ERP system as standard as possible by minimizing customizations within the core system. Instead of heavily modifying the SAP core, organizations extend functionality through side-by-side extensions, APIs, and cloud-based solutions. The approach aligns with SAP's strategic direction for S/4HANA and its cloud-first future.

At its essence, a Clean Core strategy involves:

  1. Maintaining standard SAP processes in the core ERP system
  2. Moving customizations and extensions outside the core
  3. Connecting systems through standardized APIs and integration points
  4. Leveraging the cloud for scalability and innovation

Why Clean Core Matters Now

The concept of Clean Core isn't entirely new, but it has gained tremendous momentum with SAP's shift toward cloud offerings and S/4HANA adoption. Several factors make it particularly relevant today:

The Technical Debt Burden

Many organizations running legacy SAP systems have accumulated significant technical debt through years of customizations. These modifications often create:

  • Complex upgrade paths requiring extensive testing
  • Higher maintenance costs
  • Difficulty implementing new features
  • Compatibility issues with SAP's innovation roadmap

The average SAP system contains thousands of custom code objects, with studies showing that up to 60% of this custom code is rarely or never used. This represents both waste and unnecessary complexity.

SAP's Strategic Direction

SAP has made it clear that the future lies in cloud-based solutions with standardized processes, exemplified by:

  • S/4HANA Cloud's limited customization options
  • SAP's focus on industry-specific best practices
  • The Business Technology Platform (BTP) as the primary extension environment
  • RISE with SAP's emphasis on standardization and simplification

Digital Transformation Imperatives

Modern business requires agility that traditional heavily-customized SAP implementations often can't provide:

  • Rapidly changing market conditions demand quick responses
  • Cross-functional processes need to span beyond traditional ERP boundaries
  • Emerging technologies like AI, IoT, and advanced analytics require flexible integration points
  • Talent acquisition favors modern, cloud-based technology stacks

The Four Pillars of a Clean Core Strategy

1. Core Standardization

The foundation of Clean Core is minimizing modifications to the SAP system itself. This involves:

  • Adopting SAP's standard business processes where possible
  • Removing or refactoring unnecessary customizations
  • Leveraging SAP-provided industry solutions instead of custom development
  • Implementing configuration rather than modification when business needs differ from standard

Organizations should regularly review their custom developments, measuring them against the standard functionality available in newer SAP releases. The goal isn't to eliminate all customizations but to be strategic about where and how they're implemented.

2. Side-by-Side Extensions

When standard functionality isn't sufficient, the Clean Core approach advocates building extensions outside the core system:

  • SAP Business Technology Platform (BTP) provides cloud-based extension capabilities
  • Microservices architecture allows modular, independently deployable business functions
  • Custom applications can be developed using modern frameworks and languages
  • Third-party specialized solutions can be integrated rather than building custom functionality

This approach provides significant advantages:

  • Extensions can be updated independently of the core system
  • Different technologies can be used when appropriate
  • Development cycles can be accelerated through modern DevOps practices
  • New capabilities can be added without destabilizing core business processes

3. API-First Integration

The connection between core and extensions is managed through well-defined APIs:

  • SAP API Business Hub offers thousands of standardized APIs
  • OData services provide standardized data access
  • SAP Integration Suite enables managed connectivity between systems
  • Event-driven architectures allow loose coupling between components

API-first thinking changes how development occurs by:

  • Establishing clear contracts between systems
  • Enabling parallel development of core and extensions
  • Providing governance and security for data exchange
  • Supporting backward compatibility for future upgrades

4. Intelligent Data Management

Clean Core isn't just about code; it also addresses data management:

  • Determining what data belongs in the core vs. extensions
  • Implementing data archiving strategies to maintain performance
  • Leveraging SAP HANA's capabilities for real-time analytics
  • Creating a unified data layer across core and extended systems

Benefits of a Clean Core Approach

1. Reduced Total Cost of Ownership

A Clean Core strategy can significantly reduce long-term costs through:

  • Simplified upgrades requiring less testing and remediation
  • Decreased maintenance effort for custom code
  • Lower infrastructure costs through cloud-based extensions
  • Improved resource utilization with standardized skills

Organizations typically see 20-30% reduction in upgrade costs and 15-25% reduction in ongoing maintenance after implementing Clean Core principles.

2. Increased Agility and Innovation

Clean Core enables faster response to business needs:

  • New functionality can be developed and deployed independently of core release cycles
  • Modern development frameworks accelerate implementation timeframes
  • Cloud-based extensions scale elastically with business demand
  • Experimentation becomes lower risk when isolated from core systems

3. Improved Stability and Compliance

By protecting the core, organizations experience:

  • Fewer production issues related to custom code
  • More predictable performance characteristics
  • Better alignment with SAP's security and compliance standards
  • Clearer separation of responsibilities between core and extensions

4. Future-Ready Architecture

Perhaps most importantly, Clean Core prepares organizations for what's next:

  • Smoother transition to cloud-based deployments
  • Readiness for artificial intelligence and machine learning integration
  • Ability to adopt emerging technologies without core disruption
  • Easier talent acquisition with modern technology stacks

Implementation Strategy: The Path to Clean Core

Achieving a Clean Core isn't an overnight process. Organizations typically follow a phased approach:

Phase 1: Assessment and Strategy

  • Conduct comprehensive assessment of existing customizations and extensions using Agentic AI Platforms like KTern.AI Digital Maps
  • Analyze usage patterns to identify obsolete functionality
  • Map business processes against standard SAP capabilities
  • Define the target architecture based on business priorities
  • Establish governance and decision frameworks for future development

Phase 2: Technical Debt Reduction

  • Remove unused custom code
  • Refactor essential custom functionality to use standard interfaces
  • Implement SAP standard processes where feasible
  • Identify candidates for side-by-side extensions

Phase 3: Extension Platform Development

  • Establish the technical foundation for extensions (typically SAP BTP)
  • Develop API management capabilities
  • Create reference architectures for different types of extensions
  • Accelerate the development of side-by-side Cloud Application Programming (CAP) extensions leveraging GenAI capabilities from the KTern.AI Digital Clean Core platform

Phase 4: Systematic Migration

  • Prioritize extensions based on business value and technical complexity
  • Gradually migrate functionality from core customizations to extensions
  • Implement appropriate testing to ensure business continuity
  • Decommission replaced customizations

Phase 5: Continuous Optimization

  • Monitor extension performance and usage
  • Regularly review custom code inventory
  • Evaluate new SAP standard functionality against existing extensions
  • Adjust governance policies based on lessons learned

Common Challenges and How to Overcome Them

1. Organizational Resistance

Stakeholders may resist standardization due to concerns about losing functionality or control. Address this by:

  • Demonstrating the long-term business benefits
  • Starting with non-controversial areas
  • Building early wins to establish credibility
  • Involving business users in the standardization process

2. Technical Complexity

Some customizations are deeply embedded in the core system and difficult to extract. Approaches include:

  • Progressive refactoring rather than complete rewrites
  • Temporary wrapper solutions during transition periods
  • Selective use of compatible custom code where necessary
  • Partner-supported tools for code analysis and migration

3. Skills Gaps

Clean Core requires different skills than traditional SAP development. Organizations can:

  • Invest in training for existing staff
  • Create hybrid teams with traditional and cloud skills
  • Engage partners with experience in both domains
  • Implement knowledge transfer mechanisms

4. Legacy Integration Points

Older systems may lack modern APIs or integration capabilities. Solutions include:

  • Implementing middleware for legacy system connections
  • Creating API wrappers around existing interfaces
  • Phased replacement of legacy integration points
  • Temporary dual maintenance during transition periods

Looking Forward: Clean Core and the Future of SAP

The Clean Core approach is increasingly becoming the standard for SAP implementations, with several emerging trends:

1. AI-Enhanced Extensions

Machine learning and AI capabilities are being integrated with Clean Core architectures to:

  • Automate routine business processes
  • Enhance decision-making with predictive analytics
  • Provide intelligent user experiences through chatbots and virtual assistants
  • Optimize core system performance through predictive maintenance

2. Composable Enterprise Architecture

Clean Core aligns perfectly with the concept of composable business, where:

  • Business capabilities are packaged as interchangeable building blocks
  • Organizations can quickly reconfigure processes to meet changing needs
  • Third-party solutions can be seamlessly integrated into the landscape
  • Business capabilities can be consumed as services

3. Autonomous Operations

Future Clean Core implementations will likely incorporate self-managing aspects:

  • Automatic performance optimization
  • Self-healing capabilities for common issues
  • Continuous background upgrade processes
  • Automated compliance monitoring and remediation

Conclusion: The Strategic Imperative of Clean Core

The SAP Clean Core approach represents more than just a technical architecture—it's a strategic business imperative for organizations looking to balance stability with innovation. By maintaining a standardized core and extending functionality through modern cloud-based solutions, companies can reduce costs, accelerate innovation, and position themselves for future success.

As SAP continues to evolve toward cloud-first solutions, the organizations that embrace Clean Core principles will find themselves better equipped to leverage new capabilities, respond to market changes, and maintain a competitive advantage. The path may require investment and organizational change, but the alternative—continuing to accumulate technical debt in heavily customized systems—has become increasingly unsustainable.

The journey to Clean Core is ultimately a journey toward business agility, where IT becomes an enabler rather than a constraint. For SAP customers, it represents the best path forward in an increasingly complex and fast-paced business environment.

entry.title: Re: BTP Trial account - prompted for username and pwd for...
entry.content:
Hi,

Looks like the Trust configuration is broken,

Under trial a/c->Security->Trsut Configuration, add SAP IdP(if not already done) save && activate.

Thanks

entry.title: Re: How Automated Processing Rules for Bank Statement works?
entry.content:
Hi,

Is there a way to do post with clearing of Bank Sub account (One step) using the Processing rule. In our case, the AP line is already cleared during Automatic Payment run and we want to only reconcile the Bank sub account while the corresponding Bank statement line items are processed/posted.

Thanks,

Partha

entry.title: Re: How to load Plan Via flexible upload for 12 months at...
entry.content: Thank you for response.

entry.title: VMS custom coding for custom VELO action
entry.content:
Hi Experts, I need some guideline on ABAP customization in VMS. To be frank, i have zero knowledge in VMS. I used to handle ABAP change for FI, SD, MM, WTY etc.

The functional has created 10 new custom VELO actions and half of it to create invoice (VF01) and another half to create SO (VA01). He has setup the SPRO and emailed me to proceed with the ABAP coding. Like other module, I have asked him for the exit, BAPI mapping, the linkage between VELO action and exit etc. He is not sure on that.

From googling, seems the custom changes in VMS are little bit different than other module.

I need advise and help especially the VMS step-by-step on how and where to start with this new CR.

entry.title: Re: Favorite book and SAP Community Readers?
entry.content:
I used to teach 1984 to 11th-12th graders! Mixed reviews from the teens though 😂 My favorites are:

East of Eden —got me into literature

Man’s Search for Meaning —Life changing

Their Eyes Were Watching God —a woman finds herself, beautiful use of language

Anything by Toni Morrison, usually heart breaking but her way with words is unmatched

Thanks for all the great recommendations!

entry.title: Re: How to load Plan Via flexible upload for 12 months at...
entry.content: Can you help to clarify below points 1. If all 12 months have to be opened at the same time then what is the use of the app "Define Consolidation Cycle and Assign to version" 2. In our business scenario planning numbers will be updated monthly in the planning tool which we need to bring in Group reporting for consolidation, Every month as actuals get booked they need to be consolidated with plan data for future months. How do we handle such a scenario. 3. If we keep updating the same version with new plan data then we won't have track of old plan numbers Can you please suggest how should we do the design in group reporting.

entry.title: Re: Favorite book and SAP Community Readers?
entry.content: Sounds really good! Reminds me of The 7 Habits of Highly Effective People. I will check it out, thanks!

entry.title: Re: Value of custom fields not shown in list FAGLB03
entry.content:
Hi,

Thanks for your answer.

The note is about FAGLPOSYEXT and FAGLL03. I have tried the solution in the development environment, but nothing has changed.

Is FAGLPOSX the same thing? I have tried to view FAGLPOSXEXT as equivalent, but that doesn't 'exist. In the note there is another note mentioned, number 215798. But that note refers to RFPOSXEXT. In my system, the custom fields are not in there.

entry.title: Re: add toolbar action on a table to update all rows
entry.content:
Hello,

thanks but this not what I was asking for. I know that code you have given to me is yours.

Is the coding part of your service (backend) ? But how do you implement a kind of popup in order to ask for the value(s) you like to preset in the backend and how do you call the backend from a controller function or whatever? I'm missing an example in Fiori in order to get parameter input with backend call.

entry.title: Re: How to load Plan Via flexible upload for 12 months at...
entry.content:
1. If all 12 months have to be opened at the same time then what is the use of the app "Define Consolidation Cycle and Assign to version"

=> you can run the tasks of data and consmonitor in a bundle mode for ALL periods which are in the consolidation cylcel

2. In our business scenario planning numbers will be updated monthly in the planning tool which we need to bring in Group reporting for consolidation, Every month as actuals get booked they need to be consolidated with plan data for future months. How do we handle such a scenario.

either you want to overwrite the OLD plan data or use another version for the new data

3. If we keep updating the same version with new plan data then we won't have track of old plan numbers Can you please suggest how should we do the design in group reporting.

refer to 2.

entry.title: Re: add toolbar action on a table to update all rows
entry.content:
See https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/guides/providing-services#actions-functions

Implement an on handler on server side.

 

Sample:

https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-cap-samples/blob/339c87c55a46b7947a0524b4d0adbb943ba1edc8/bookshop/srv/cat-service.cds#L14

https://github.com/SAP-samples/cloud-cap-samples/blob/339c87c55a46b7947a0524b4d0adbb943ba1edc8/bookshop/srv/cat-service.js#L14

entry.title: Re: SBO Mailer Configuration
entry.content:
Hi salah35 

Kindly refer to the attached SAP Notes.

2417991 - Fail to Send Email with Job Service

3363384 How To Configure SAP Business One Mailer with Gmail

3439584 - How to configure SAP Business One Mailer with Microsoft account

Kind Regards
Nidhi Singh
SAP Business One Support

entry.title: Employee Central Global Benefits - Child Allowance
entry.content:
Hi Team,

 We have a requirement to provide child allowance ( via pay component say X) whenever there is a child birth. We created an allowance in benefits with manual enrollment and integrated with payroll i.e once enrolled and approved, it gets updated in Employee's compensation information which is given monthly till child reaches 18 years. 

The benefit schedule is created till 31.12.9990

If there is another child birth of the same employee after few years, how this would work. They can't edit the same enrollment right? Should we create multiple child allowance benefits with different pay components? How this can be achieved?

Dependent specific rule doesn't work since EC benefits cannot check for which dependent the child birth is being enrolled since it cannot identify it from Dependents portlet, so cannot be checked during eligibility. 

Please let me know your thoughts.

entry.title: Re: Error no. L9029 is raised during qRFC processing from EWM
entry.content: Hello, did you find a solution? I faced the same problem.. (

entry.title: Re: ADT 3.48 bug - Delete and Ctrl-Z keys are deactivated...
entry.content: Do you also have problems navigating between tabs? So with Ctrl+PageUp/Down? It no longer works properly for me. The navigation direction is mixed up and in classes it jumps to the “Test Classes” sub-tab. Does anyone else have this problem? I have reinstalled Eclipse and the ADTs and before installing the ATDs the navigation still worked. Afterwards not anymore!

entry.title: Re: Error E:EDOCUMENT_MY:017 when posting DEBMAS idocs
entry.content:
Hi,

The issue is solved. In the upgrade, (new) customer screen groups are added by default, in our case MY (Malaysia), TW (Taiwan) and UA (Ukraine). In earlier upgrade also VS was added.

I have deleted those 4 screen groups, we don't use them. That solved the issue. The error message related to MY was not the real error, that was related to tax.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Transition from Standalone to Embedded Deployment in SAP S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi. 

We are following the updated procedure for migrating to embedded and are now running in parallell. What is the best practice when it comes to doing development and handle the Fiori development objects in both the hub and embedded while running in parallell? 

BR

Sveinung

entry.title: Enterprise Project ID Validation via BAdI /S4PPM/PROJECT_VALIDATION
entry.content:
In SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition, it's often necessary to ensure that the Project ID format aligns with the assigned Project Type or Project Profile. For example, a project with type "Training" should start with "H", and a project with profile "Statistical Project" should start with "S".

In this blog post, I show how to implement validation logic using the BAdI  /S4PPM/PROJECT_VALIDATION to enforce such naming rules. If the Project ID does not match the expected pattern for its type or profile, a custom error message will prevent project creation.

Use case

We want to validate that the first character of the Project ID corresponds to the Project Type or Project Profile, for example:

Mapping by Project Type

Project IDProject TypeProject Type Description
A*26Local Investments
B*34BDM
C*35Marketing
D*36Proposal & Prospection
E*40Admin General
F*41Recruiting
G*42Local Meetings
H*46Training
J*47FW Meetings

Mapping by Project Profile

Project IDProject Profile CodeProject Profile Description
R*YP05Project with Revenue
S*YP04Statistical Project

Prerequisites

Ensure that your user has the following roles assigned:

Business Role Business Role ID
Extensibility SpecialistSAP_BR_EXTENSIBILITY_SPEC
Project Financial ControllerSAP_BR_PROJ_FIN_CONTROLLER
 
If you don’t have the necessary roles, please contact your administrator or refer to the Maintain Business Users app.

Implementation

To implement the Project ID validation, you’ll create a new custom logic implementation based on the extension point /S4PPM/PROJECT_VALIDATION, configure the necessary parameters, and insert the validation code that enforces your naming rules.

1. Open the Custom Logic app (F6957)

 

Click "Create"

2. Set Extension Point 

In the opened window, set the Extension Point to /S4PPM/PROJECT_VALIDATION and select Step 2.

3. Set Implementation ID

Fill in the required fields:

Implementation Description – for example, PROJECTIDVALIDATION

Implementation ID – for example, YY1_PROJECTIDVALIDATION

and select Review.

4. Create a New Implementation

In the opened window select "Create".

5. Publish BadI Implementation

In the opened window, wait until the BAdI implementation becomes available, then select “Publish”

6. Open Code Editor

Wait until the BAdI implementation status changes to “Published”, then select “Open Code Editor”.

7. Insert the Code

In the opened window, switch to edit mode by selecting “Edit”.

Insert the сode below and select “Save and Publish”.

**********************************************************************
* Validate Project ID Format Based on Project Type or Profile Code
* Mapping Rules:
* A* → Type 26   (Local Investments)
* B* → Type 34   (BDM)
* C* → Type 35   (Marketing)
* D* → Type 36   (Proposal & Prospection)
* E* → Type 40   (Admin General)
* F* → Type 41   (Recruiting)
* G* → Type 42   (Local Meetings)
* H* → Type 46   (Training)
* J* → Type 47   (FW Meetings)
* R* → Profile YP05
* S* → Profile YP04
**********************************************************************
 DATA: lv_naming_ok TYPE abap_bool VALUE abap_false.

  CLEAR: validationmessages.
  
* Validate Project ID prefix against Project Type
  IF enterpriseproject-project CP 'A*' AND enterpriseproject-enterpriseprojecttype CP '26'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
  ELSEIF enterpriseproject-project CP 'B*' AND enterpriseproject-enterpriseprojecttype CP '34'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
  ELSEIF enterpriseproject-project CP 'C*' AND enterpriseproject-enterpriseprojecttype CP '35'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
  ELSEIF enterpriseproject-project CP 'D*' AND enterpriseproject-enterpriseprojecttype CP '36'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
  ELSEIF enterpriseproject-project CP 'E*' AND enterpriseproject-enterpriseprojecttype CP '40'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
  ELSEIF enterpriseproject-project CP 'F*' AND enterpriseproject-enterpriseprojecttype CP '41'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
  ELSEIF enterpriseproject-project CP 'G*' AND enterpriseproject-enterpriseprojecttype CP '42'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
  ELSEIF enterpriseproject-project CP 'H*' AND enterpriseproject-enterpriseprojecttype CP '46'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
  ELSEIF enterpriseproject-project CP 'J*' AND enterpriseproject-enterpriseprojecttype CP '47'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
    
* Validate Project ID prefix against Project Profile Code    
  ELSEIF enterpriseproject-project CP 'R*' AND enterpriseproject-projectprofilecode CP 'YP05'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
  ELSEIF enterpriseproject-project CP 'S*' AND enterpriseproject-projectprofilecode CP 'YP04'.
    lv_naming_ok = abap_true.
  ENDIF.
  
**********************************************************************
* Raise validation error if no matching rule found
**********************************************************************

  IF lv_naming_ok = abap_false.
    APPEND INITIAL LINE TO validationmessages ASSIGNING FIELD-SYMBOL(<ls_validationmessage>).
    <ls_validationmessage>-systemmessageclass     = '/S4PPM/VAL_DET'.
    <ls_validationmessage>-systemmessagetype      = 'E'.
    <ls_validationmessage>-systemmessagenumber    = '000'.
    <ls_validationmessage>-systemmessagevariable1 = 'First character of the project id'.
    <ls_validationmessage>-systemmessagevariable2 = 'shall correspond project type/project profile.'  .
    <ls_validationmessage>-systemmessagevariable3 = ' Please use correct project id'    .
  ENDIF.

Test Example

To test the custom logic, open the “Project Control – Enterprise Projects” app (F3215).

Create a new Enterprise Project by selecting “Create”.

Fill in all the mandatory fields (Project Name, Project ID, Project Profile, Planned Start, Planned Finish, Responsible Cost Center, Profit Center) to create a new Enterprise Project.

For the Project Type, select a value that is not aligned with the initial character of the Project ID, based on the mapping rules defined earlier — for example, choose Local Investments (26) and enter a Project ID starting with “B”, which does not correspond to the expected prefix for that type. Try to save the project by selecting “Create”.

An error message defined earlier in the Custom Logic will appear.

Now let’s test the validation logic for the Project ID – Project Profile mapping.
Select Statistical Project as the Project Profile, and enter a Project ID that starts with “R” — this combination does not align with the mapping rules defined earlier.
Try to save the project by selecting “Create”.

We see the error message again.

Summary

In this blog post, we looked how to implement custom validation logic for Enterprise Project IDs in SAP S/4HANA Cloud using the BAdI /S4PPM/PROJECT_VALIDATION.
By defining simple mapping rules between Project ID prefixes and either Project Type or Project Profile, we ensured that projects follow internal naming standards.

We walked through the implementation steps using the Custom Logic app, inserted the validation code, and tested the logic through the Project Control – Enterprise Projects app.

This approach helps enforce data consistency and governance in the Public Cloud.

Let me know if you’ve implemented similar validation scenarios!

Mohammed

entry.title: Sales Order Total is Zero
entry.content:
There are some sales orders available in the system, they contain item line with pricing information, but the document value is 0. You want to know the reason.


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Sales Order Total is Zero
entry.content:
The cancelled order line is not considerred in the document total as well as the preview output. 

If the sales order is cancelled entirely, then the order total is zero.

reference KBAs:

3221500 - Canceled Item Is Not Displayed in Sales Quote and Sales Order Preview

3585895 - The Partially Cancelled Line Item is Not Displayed in Sales Order Preview

3595519 - Sales Order Total is Zero When the Order is Fully Cancelled

Thanks,

Adie

entry.title: Re: ArchiveLink connection issue: ERROR => NiBufIConnect:...
entry.content:
Hi
Do you have any Firewall or NAT or Load Balancer between you SAP server and OpenText?
If yes, please check how the source port is setup on SNAT (this is hint)

Regards
Alex

entry.title: Re: ADT 3.48 bug - Delete and Ctrl-Z keys are deactivated...
entry.content: @ptrck I navigate via Ctrl+E (AKA Ctrl+F6). If I try Ctrl+Page Down and Page Up, it seems to work the same before and after pressing Ctrl+F.

entry.title: The Death of Traditional ERP?
entry.content:
The Death of Traditional ERP?


For decades, ERP systems have been the backbone of enterprise operations, managing everything from finance and supply chain to HR and procurement. But as technology evolves, traditional monolithic ERP models are being challenged by more flexible, connected, and AI-driven solutions.

Is this the end of traditional ERP as we know it? Or is it simply evolving?

 Why Traditional ERP is Fading

Legacy ERP systems have long suffered from:
❌Rigid architectures that make upgrades costly and time-consuming.
❌Siloed data structures that limit cross-functional insights.
❌High implementation costs that deter smaller enterprises from adoption.

In today’s fast-moving business environment, companies need agility, real-time insights, and AI-driven decision-making—capabilities that traditional on-premise ERP often lacks.

 The Rise of Composable and Cloud-Based ERP

Modern enterprises are shifting to composable, cloud-based architectures that allow greater flexibility and scalability. SAP S/4HANA Cloud is a prime example of how ERP is transforming:

✅Modular and composable architecture – Companies can implement only the components they need.
✅Real-time data processing – Leveraging SAP Business Data Cloud, organizations can unify data sources for better analytics.
✅AI-Embedded Workflows – With tools like SAP Joule, ERP is no longer just transactional but predictive and automated.

How Low-Code and AI Are Reshaping ERP

The shift away from traditional ERP is further accelerated by:

🔹Low-code and no-code platforms like SAP Build, allowing business users to develop applications without heavy IT involvement.
🔹AI-driven automation through SAP Business AI, enabling proactive decision-making rather than reactive problem-solving.
🔹Connected ecosystems with SAP Integration Suite, allowing ERP to seamlessly integrate with other enterprise systems.

 What’s Next? ERP’s Evolution, Not Its End

ERP is not disappearing—it is transforming into a smarter, more connected digital core. Companies must:

✅Embrace cloud and AI to make ERP more intelligent.
✅Adopt a modular approach for greater flexibility.
✅Leverage real-time data for proactive business insights.

So, is traditional ERP dead? Not quite. It’s simply evolving into something more powerful. The real question is: Is your business evolving with it?

What’s your take? Is the ERP model as we know it coming to an end?

entry.title: Re: SAP Community All Time - 47 to 50 is missing or is it reserved numbers ??
entry.content: What's the URL?

entry.title: Re: BTP Trial account - prompted for username and pwd for ABAP env from BTP and from Eclipse
entry.content: Default life of BTP Trial account is 3 months. Could it be the "problem"?

entry.title: Special GL indicator field/Using Alternative Account for Supplier in Create supplier invoice App
entry.content:
• According to SAP Note 2856664, the Create Incoming Invoices app does not offer park functionality, and the resolution provided is to use the Fiori app Create Supplier Invoice (App ID F0859) for parking supplier invoices.

I understand that in the Create Supplier Invoice app, invoices can be created without reference to a Purchase Order, and we are able to select Document Type in the details section, as also recommended by the J60 Test Script.

However, I am facing a few issues with this app in SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud:

- There is no field for Special G/L Indicator (e.g. for down payments or other special postings), which limits our ability to handle some specific business cases.

- It is not possible to change the G/L Account (Reconciliation Account) of the supplier line item. In some scenarios, we would like to use an alternative account (different from the reconciliation account in the supplier master data), similar to the flexibility provided in the Create Outgoing Invoice (FB60) app in the On-Premise version.

While I still want to park the document, I find these limitations blocking the process.

Could you please advise:

  • If there is any workaround  for including the Special G/L Indicator field in the Create Supplier Invoice app?
  • If it's possible to manually assign a different G/L Account to the supplier line item in this app (perhaps via configuration or extension)?

  • And finally, is there a way to park such documents (with Special G/L or custom G/L Account) in the current cloud setup?

Looking forward to your guidance.

entry.title: CLT: Copy country by itself
entry.content:
Hi Experts, 

 

Out of curiosity, can we copy an existing country as itself and change the country currency? e.g. SG -> SG

My ask is because we have this requirement that the functional currency is not the same as local currency, in Public Cloud we are not able to change the local currency. When we presented to them the solution of additional currency, they don't want to accept it because of the inherent issue due to exchange rate differences. Particularly cost estimate and production cost are all costed in local currency and not in functional currency aggravating more on the exchange rate differences. 

Regards, 

Derek

entry.title: Re: SAP ASE HADR - database replication limit
entry.content:
Hello Chris,

thank you for a comprehensive response. 

As for the mirrored HW config of both nodes, this is also our goal.

As for the client application being certified for replication with HADR, this what also have on mind with the client as e.g. we know the app utilizes XA transactions which have some replicaction restrictions as per https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_ASE/efe56ad3cad0467d837c8ff1ac6ba75c/5c60768b0f894deeb37aa2d6b1cb31bf.html?&version=16.0.4.2

XA transaction replication is supported with exceptions of the following scenarios:

  • XA transactions executed cross multiple SAP ASE databases cannot be replicated.

  • HADR system with external replication.

  • HADR system with a DR node.

Replication of transactions that are coordinated by MSDTC is not supported in HADR.



However I am not entirely sure on the meaning of these two

  • XA transactions executed cross multiple SAP ASE databases cannot be replicated.
  • HADR system with a DR node.

 

Regarding the first one, does it pertain to transactions spanning databases hosted on different ASE instances, or maybe databases residing on the same instance ?

Regarding the second one, does it mean a setup with in which the cluster is set up with async replication to the companion node with the use of Simple Persistent Queue ? Because this is how I understand it:

If you set up ASE HADR as a two-node cluster with sync replication, you effectively set it up as a HA solution because you have real-time replication with the so-called ZDL (zero data loss).

If you set up up ASE HADR as a two-node cluster with async replication, you effectively set it up as a DR solution because the replicated data is first buffered in the SPQ on the primary node and if it goes down, the companion node may not have this data.

Please correct me if I am wrong as I am new to replication in ASE.

entry.title: SAP CPI Message Mapping – Filter and Align Business Partner names correctly per line item
entry.content:
Hi,

I'm working in SAP Integration Suite on a transformation/message mapping scenario where I need to transform repeating lines into individual records in the target.

My Goal:

  • For each <LineItem>, select the correct BusinessPartner name in the correct order

  • The correct name can appear in either <Name1> or <Name2> depending on other logic.

  • If either value is equal to "CompanyX", it should be excluded

  • If names are null/empty, I want to output the string "Empty"

Example Input (Simplified):

<Document>
<MessageHeader>
<Header>

<LineItem>
<Linedetails>
<InvolvedParties>
<Party>
<Buyer>
<Name1>CompanyX</Name1>
</Buyer>
<Seller>
<Name2>Company1</Name2>
</Seller>
</Party>
</InvolvedParties>
</Linedetails>
</LineItem>

<LineItem>
<Linedetails>
<InvolvedParties>
<!-- No Party/Name elements -->
</InvolvedParties>
</Linedetails>
</LineItem>

<LineItem>
<Linedetails>
<InvolvedParties>
<Party>
<Seller>
<Name1>Company2</Name1>
</Seller>
<Buyer>
<Name2>CompanyX</Name2>
</Buyer>
</Party>
</InvolvedParties>
</Linedetails>
</LineItem>

</Header>
</MessageHeader>
</Document>

NOTE: CompanyX should never be sent to the output.

The output i get:

<NewLineItem>
<BusinessPartner>Company1</BusinessPartner>
</NewLineItem>
<NewLineItem>
<BusinessPartner>Company2</BusinessPartner>
</NewLineItem>
<NewLineItem>
<BusinessPartner>Empty</BusinessPartner>
</NewLineItem>

Expected Output:

<NewLineItem>
<BusinessPartner>Company1</BusinessPartner>
</NewLineItem>
<NewLineItem>
<BusinessPartner>Empty</BusinessPartner>
</NewLineItem>
<NewLineItem>
<BusinessPartner>Company2</BusinessPartner>
</NewLineItem>

What I Tried:
- A Groovy UDF that takes in Name1, Name2, and a third "anchor" value to trigger per-row execution

Despite this, the output becomes misaligned: either names are skipped, appear in the wrong order, or "Empty" shows up at the wrong line.

What’s the best approach in CPI Message Mapping to:

  • Process each <LineItem> individually with the correct data in correct order? i.e. when fields are empty/null, it wont take the next available value to populate the field.

  • Ensure correct row alignment and output order

  • Return a default "Empty" if both names are excluded

Any help, especially from those working with multi-row mappings or filtering per record, would be really appreciated.

entry.title: Re: CU50 in internal tables
entry.content: S 00344 : No batch input data for screen SAPLCEI0 0109. That's a screen with title "Configuration: Batch Input". Could you run Batch Input CU50 with the option NOBINPT = 'X' please?

entry.title: Re: How to automatically create a Job Information event a...
entry.content:
Hello Nicoleta,

The "Garden Leave date" field in Termination Details portlet is indeed manually populated. So far, we don't have anything configured (it's a future need), but the goal is to have an entry in Job Information with an event reason "Garden Leave" (that has the event "Data Change") at the date that was put in the field "Garden Leave date".

At first I took inspiration to the blog article Automatic Absence Creation for Late coming/Early leaving in SuccessFactors Time Tracking to have a LOA created when we put a value in the field "Garden Leave date", and it worked, but the need is to have a data change entry in Job Information, not a Leave of Absence. I've then tried to create an Integration Center job to create a Job Information entry, but IC with scenario from ISC event "Employee Termination" don't have the Job Information entity available to upsert data. 

Thank you for the link on cross-entity business rules. If I understand it well, active employees (since they are being terminated in a future date) can't have cross-entity rules sourced in Employment Details with target Job Information, so the Business Rule solution is out of the question.

 

Regards,

William Blanco

entry.title: Re: Merge PDF of type TLINE
entry.content: You're plain wrong in thinking that 2 PDF can be merged by just concatenating their bytes. You need special code to merge 2 PDF files; there's one class to do that, which you can find by searching the forum. Another solution is to merge the two ABAP List spool files into one ABAP List spool file before calling CONVERT_ABAPSPOOLJOB_2_PDF once.

entry.title: Variable Pay search user options missing
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am not sure why but maybe you can help me out. I am missing details when I am trying to search for users in Variable Pay

This screenshot is from our own internal Test system, in a Salesdemo environment I am used to have more options to select and search for users.

Maybe someone can give me a hint, maybe I forgot something?

Michael

entry.title: Re: How do you Mass Change field values in S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content: Hi experts,

entry.title: Re: Preparing for SAP Identity Management’s End-of-Maintenance in 2027
entry.content:
Hi Tony, 

moving from SAP Identity Management to Microsoft Entra or another IAM solution usually is not a direct automatic migration but involves reviewing the landscape, the workflows and configuration and adapting that to the new solution.

As such, this also works if you start with SAP Identity Management 7.2. And I wouldn't recommend to upgrade to 8.0 first, but skip this step and directly move to the intended target architecture.

Best,

Michael

entry.title: Re: How do you Mass Change field values in S/4HANA Cloud?

entry.title: Re: 🧰👷‍♂️ The SAP S/4HANA RISE & SAP BTP - Toolbox 👷‍♂️🧰
entry.content:
Security Security Security on RISE & ECS

This is another excellent presentation from  Jana Subramanian and this deserves a round of applause  👏 👏 👏

RISE with SAP S/4HANA Cloud, private edition Security and Compliance

I am going to elaborate a lot of slides because these are the kind of slides which really help a SAP Technical & Security Architect to discuss these subjects with wider audiences and it's a shame to have such beautiful slides hidden away from view in a presentation, here we go...

S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Deployment Models

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Tenancy Model

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Account Boundary

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Shared Security Responsibility Model

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Shared Security Governance

 

SAP Cloud Services Security Activity

 

SAP Cloud Services Security Activity

 

SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition RISE ECS Secure Connectivity

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition RISE ECS Short Distance DR Disaster Recovery

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition RISE ECS Long Distance DR Disaster Recovery

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition RISE Landscape

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition RISE ECS Secure Data Flow

 

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition RISE ECS Managed Security Firewall Service

 

SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition RISE ECS Application and Infrastructure Logs

 

SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition RISE ECS LogServ Service

 

SAP S/4HANA Private Cloud Edition RISE ECS Security Vulnerability Advisory Services

entry.title: Validation on Hire Rule
entry.content:
Hello Community!

I need to create a validation (business rule) in accrual when hiring in Successfactors.

This accrual can only be created if employee joins the first working day of the month.

The first working day is not necessarily a Monday. For example, if Monday and Tuesday are holidays, the work week starts on Wednesday.

I don't know how to do it.

Please, advise me.

entry.title: Re: SAP Document and Reporting Compliance for Singapore Peppol eInvoices (InvoiceNow) - Part 2
entry.content: @PranavSanyo as I checked with development team, "IS relevant" could cause from different issues so they suggested to raise an incident so they can help better. Please raise incident with component "CA-GTF-CSC-EDO-SG".

entry.title: Key Highlights from the 1H 2025 Release of SuccessFactors Onboarding
entry.content:
Dear Community ,

Kindly find 1H2025 Key features for Onboarding Module :

1.Alumni Experience :

The Alumni Experience gives former employees access to their personal and payroll related information after their employment ends. Kindly find below Blog for more details.

Alumni Experience- 1H2025 Release - SAP Community

2.Document Migration from ONB1.0 to ONB2.0 :

As an Onboarding 1.0 administrator, you can now migrate all your documents to the latest Onboarding solution before the sunset of Onboarding 1.0. Kindly refer below blog for more details.

Preparing for the Future: Onboarding 1.0 Document ... - SAP Community

3.Enhancement in Onboarding Process for Internal Hires:

Switch 1:Enable Enhanced Internal Hire Process- Will be Automatically ON from 1H2025 release.(Universal)

Switch 2: Enable Additional Internal Hire Activities- It is added to support functionalities such as workflow support, cross-entity rule support, and position updates which were not available with switch 1.

Kindly refer below blog for more details.

2H 2024 SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding enhancement ... - SAP Community

4.Enhancement to Onboarding Integration Setup Page:

You can now select a new option called Apply Onboarding to Job Requisitions via Business Rule to Define Eligibility for Onboarding Initiation from the Onboarding Integration Setup page and configure a dedicated business rule to define the eligibility for onboarding initiation.

New Appplicant Management Experience should be turned ON to utilize this feature.

5.Simplified Print Forms Service Credentialing and Provisioning :

P-User ID is no longer required to enable PFS, removing the need to renew its password every six months.

The actual transition is planned in the preview environment in a phased manner starting from 5 May, 2025. To check the status of transition, kindly refer below blog for more details

Simplifying Print Form Services Credentialling - SAP Community

6.Enhanced Document Flow Support for Corrections after Final Review Step:

System generates a new version of the document in Document Flow each time data correction is requested in Final Review.

7.Custom Tasks Step Attachments available in New Hire Details:

Documents uploaded to custom tasks are now visible from Onboarding Documents in the New Hire Details page.

8.Change in Legal Entity Transfer Flow :

Employees who are transferred on Legal Entity Transfer can't begin their new employment on the day of their employment unless Manage Pending Hire is completed.

9.AI Assisted Writing For Nudge Feature in Onboarding :For Onboarding, you can now use the AI-assisted writing tool on below areas:

The Nudge Assignee Quick Action on the home page.
The Nudge button in new hire tasks on the New Hire Details page.

Kindly refer Release information Link for more details :https://help.sap.com/docs/successfactors-release-information/8e0d540f96474717bbf18df51e54e522/03aea6c460d34548b72fdaee5cda56e4.html

Thanks,
Gopinath Murugaiyan
IT Technology Senior Consultant
Workforce Solutions | Corporate Functions & Analytics.
SAP Labs India Private Limited | Whitefield | Bangalore- 560066, India.

entry.title: Indian numbering system (i.e., Crore, Lakh, Thousand) in SAP Business One (SAP B1H)
entry.content:
The Indian numbering system (i.e., Crore, Lakh, Thousand) in SAP Business One (SAP B1H) environment. This feature would greatly enhance clarity and usability for users who are accustomed to this regional format, especially in financial and reporting modules.

Currently, the system displays numbers in the standard international format (Millions/Billions), which can be less intuitive for our Indian finance and operations teams. is there any existing localization pack, configuration setting, or customization available to enable this feature?

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud – Sales Order Image Attachments on A...
entry.content:
Dear Taqi,

Following can be one of solution:

  • Create Fiori app
  • Get Sales order from CDS->OData, to expose the CDS as Service below URL may be reffered:
    • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-sap/exposing-an-odata-service-from-sap-s-4hana-cloud-public-edition-to-the-sap/ba-p/13628248 
  • Prepare your formatted form in HTML where you may also add image tag
  • Use “window.print()” as described in below link to print and save as PDF
    • https://community.sap.com/t5/technology-blogs-by-members/printing-digital-form-in-sapui5-sap-fiori/ba-p/13357965 

Regards,

Iftikhar

entry.title: Troubleshooting Access to a Specific Application Server in SAPGUI for Java
entry.content:
Who might find this helpful?

  • Anyone who has just installed SAPGUI for Java

  • First-time SAPGUI for Java users

  • Anyone who’s encountered similar connection errors in SAPGUI for Java

Introduction

I had been using the Windows version of SAPGUI for a while, but after switching my device to a Mac, I needed to start using SAPGUI for Java. Although I was able to install it on my company device, I ran into trouble connecting to a specific application server.

This post details the simple mistake I made and how I fixed it. Hopefully, it will help you if you’re facing the same issue.

What’s the issue?

I needed to access the following server details. On SAPGUI for Windows, I could simply enter the values in each field without any problems:

  • Application Server: WW.XXXX.YYY.ZZZ

  • Instance: 00

  • System ID (SID): SAP

Note: These connection details are only examples (placeholders) for illustration.

However, in SAPGUI for Java, entering the information in the same way resulted in an error.

Where did I go wrong?

Initially, I typed “SAP” into the System ID field, saved the settings, and tried to connect—only to see a list of globally registered SIDs instead of accessing the server I wanted.

This was the mistake. The co nfiguration process in SAPGUI for Java differs slightly compared to the Windows version, causing my error.

Solution: Use Expert Mode and enter the settings directly

1. Go to “Details” and enable Expert Mode

In the connection settings for SAPGUI for Java, click “Details” and switch on Expert Mode.

2. Manually enter the connection details

Use the following format:

 conn=/H/<IP address>/S/3200

  • Replace <IP address> with your target server’s IP address.

  • /S/3200 corresponds to instance “00.” Adjust the port as necessary for different instances.

Be sure to type this in manually. Copying and pasting can introduce hidden characters or line breaks, especially on a Mac, which may cause errors.

If you encounter an error like the one below, first suspect that your manual input might not be correct and verify each character.

Summary

If your usual approach from SAPGUI for Windows doesn’t work in SAPGUI for Java, remember to try Expert Mode and manually input the connection details. A small tweak like this can go a long way in ensuring a successful connection.

I hope this helps anyone dealing with SAPGUI for Java connection errors. Thanks for reading, and best of luck with your setup!

entry.title: Different ways to initiate onboarding for an internal hire.
entry.content:
When you discuss the onboarding process one of the most complex topics is how to handle internal hire onboarding or crossboarding as it is commonly called.  In this article we will look at the different options available.  We will focus more on the high-level processes rather than the detailed configuration required to achieve each step. 

To discuss this topic, we are going to set the following SuccessFactors landscape.  We are assuming the Employee Central is being used, with position management and event reason derivation. SuccessFactors recruitment and onboarding is being used and operates in three countries:

  • Germany has two legal entities.
  • United Kingdom has three legal entities
  • United States have one legal entity

As internal hire process can be complicated it is imperative that before you discuss it you need to have agreed your ‘New Hire Process’.  This is very useful as it allows you to form the baseline process for all internal hire conversation. Let’s assume the New Hire process is as follows

  1. Personal Data Collection including custom MDFs in a hire template
  2. Document signature
  3. Compliance (if applicable)
  4. MPH

There are some tasks, but these are not mandatory

We will talk about lots of different ways you can trigger internal hire onboarding processes, some may not be workable in some organizations but this article will hopefully help customers and partners to discuss the options available and make the best informed decision.

Understanding ‘how’ internal hires can move within the organization.

Before you start to discuss the internal hiring processes you need to confirm with payroll what is the impact of an internal move  on payroll.  In our example it has confirmed that in Germany you can switch between the legal entities without the need to have a termination and a hire record.  What this means if a person can switch positions in different legal entities with a simple ‘transfer’ event reason.  However, in the UK the only way a person can move between two legal entities is to terminate them on one and hire them into another entity.  This is dictated by payroll based on how the legal entities have been set up in UK, (this is just in our example),  In USA there is only one legal entity so this makes things simpler.

We have also confirmed that organization offer one way country transfer and concurrent employment. They don’t offer global assignments.

With this information we now know ‘how’ a person can move within the organization.  Now we can consider how the process is ‘Initiated’

Understand the different ways an internal hire process can be initiated

From SuccessFactors Recruitment.

This is the classic leading practice hiring process.  A job is advertised, an employee applies, they are selected, have an approved offer and ready to be hired.

If an internal hire has ‘Onboarding Initiated’ from recruitment then this will insert a future date row directly on the employee’s job information record in Employee Central.

The event reason will be defined by the event reason derivation logic and should that event reason have an associated workflow, the action will be subject to a pending approval.  Note they do not go into MPH!

Once the workflow is approved then an internal hire onboarding process can be triggered based on the specific process variant.  This process variant can be same as the New Hire process or different.

With this approach the employee central record is updated and this can sometimes create a challenge for some customers, in that do they have all the correct data in recruitment to update the record accurately.  Given that the record is already updated, how much of the ‘New Hire Process’ is relevant for the internal hire.  Can we assume that the persons employee central data is already up to date?  Do they need to sign any forms?  Remember this process can’t be used in UK for people switching legal entities.  This just leave tasks, and how relevant are these?  If someone is moving from one position to another and the manager doesn’t change, do you need any onboarding tasks? Hence it is worth spending time to consider what is needed from a data and process perspective for an internal hire.  Remember you may choose not to initiate an onboarding process.

The main downside of this process is twofold, not everyone applies for a job and some customers want more control before employee central is updated.

From Manage Pending hires

Another option is to send the candidate directly from recruitment to MPH, there is a great article written by my two colleagues on how to do this here .  The result is they are sent from recruitment to MPH.  When you process them from MPH, you can see the full EC record, you can make additional changes subject to RBP if required, validate the data and when comfortable submit the record.  The system will trigger event reason derivation and the associated workflow.  Once the workflow is approved an onboarding process can be triggered just like we described above.  Again, all the questions above about what should be in the onboarding process still apply.

This process is often overlooked but should be considered for customers who may want move control over the data before an onboarding process is triggered.

‘Managed Move’ from Employee central

However, we all know that not all internal moves come from recruitment, people can be promoted, transferred without going through recruitment.

This is what we we call a ‘Managed move’, a person moves from one position to another.  This can be done directly in employee central via ‘Take Action’, based on the event reason derivation, this will trigger a workflow and once this approved it can trigger an internal hire onboarding process. 

Again you need to discuss what in needed in a specific onboarding process for such a move.

Manually starting onboarding

In our example employees in the UK can’t switch between legal entities with a transfer event reason they need to have a hire and termination record.  For those who don’t come from recruitment you do have the option of manually starting onboarding.  This will start an onboarding process and once the rehire check has been conducted (providing they have a future dated termination) the hire can use the employment switcher to complete their data.  This process can be expanded for country transfers, and is particular useful as it allows the employee to have their record tied together and complete whilst employed in their current role.

Concurrent employment.

You can now trigger an onboarding process for concurrent employment, whether sent from recruitment or manually added in Employee Central.  Once the Employee Central record have been saved and approved an onboarding process can we initiated.

Global Assignments

These can also be used to trigger an onboarding process.

In all of the examples above I have talked about event reason derivation and workflow.  The workflow is not required this is just part of the EC process.

Hopefully this article highlights the different ways you can trigger an internal onboarding process and based on this allow you to decide on the what steps are needed in the each specific process.

entry.title: Re: commitment amount not show in S_ALR_87013558
entry.content: From ME23n on the PO created, what do you see when you navigate through Options: Environment, AC commitment documents?

entry.title: Re: Reset ANLC to rebuild a depreciation area - S/4 HANA...
entry.content:
Hey Jeremy,
thank you for your answer. I set the customizing as you recommended.

Unfortunately the report is not available in SAP S4 Private Cloud. I have the following options besides the TAC OABL (which I don't want to use in the productive system).

I have over 2000 assets which needs to be reseted. Maybe you or someone else knows an alternative?

best regards.

Marleen

entry.title: Re: Value of custom fields not shown in list FAGLB03
entry.content:
Read also 2991061 - Problem in adding special fields to Transaction FAGLL03 and good old 984305 - Line item: Definition of special fields (T021S) 

  • Did you add your fields in include structure CI_FAGLPOSX?
  • Did you check for Transport request not imported or with error(s) in the import log?
  • RFPOSX* structure are related to classic GL detail reports FBL*N.

entry.title: Questions regarding GRC 12.0 system conversion - NetWeaver 7.52 to S/4HANA 2023 Foundation
entry.content:
Questions regarding GRC 12.0 conversion (NetWeaver 7.52 to S/4HANA 2023 Foundation).

Dear All,

we are planning an system conversion for our GRC system which is running on NW7.52 to S/4HANA 2023 Foundation and I have the following questions around it. Please check and let me know.

1) Where can I find the details which highlights the advantages(New features) for moving from NetWeaver 7.52 to S/4HANA 2023 Foundation for my GRC 12.0 system.

2)  I see the GRC foundation component changing from V1200 to V8100. What is the impact of moving to V8100. Is there a SAP note explaining it.

3) I would also like check if there is a possibility of retaining the GRC foundation component V1200 intact even after conversion to S/4HANA 2023 Foundation.

Thanks,

Vijay Francis

entry.title: Re: SAC - Measure percentage difference
entry.content: Perfect, It works! Thanks

entry.title: Re: Upgrade Your Tools for Modern ECMAScript in UI5
entry.content:
Hi Merlin, 

It seems after upgrading to latest cli tools. I have error in my ui5.yaml (specVersion:3.1)and my i18n file doesn't load anymore. I upgraded to latest 4.0 which I can see in my package.json file in both MTA project and the UI5 app folder.

my package.json : 

"@ui5/cli": "^4.0.14",

i18n file not accessible anymore as it cannot reach the url to load ui5-tooling. The app is working fine but I'm not able to use i18n file..
@merlin_beutlberger @gregorw @WouterLemaire : Kindly share if you have any insights on this. Thanks 🙂

entry.title: Error "Your post has been adjusted to remove unsupported HTML found in the message body"
entry.content:
Hello!

I am trying to include a table with merged cells in a blog post. However, when I try to save a draft of the post, I encounter an error: "Your post has been adjusted to remove unsupported HTML found in the message body," and the merge operation is deleted. Has anyone had the same issue?

Best regards

entry.title: Re: Value of custom fields not shown in list FAGLB03
entry.content:
Thanks for the reply.

I think the definition of the custom fields in FAGLPOSX is not the problem. In our acceptance environment it is working fine, only production is not showing values in the list. In the FAGLPOSX the custom fields with the missing values are included. I don't see any difference between acceptance and production there.

I seems to be an issue with showing the values in the list, the values seems to be filled in the view.

entry.title: Re: SAP Fiori Overview Page - Analytic Cards - Part 1
entry.content:
HI Prasita,

If we try column chart using same annotations which is mentioned in the column chart. On Runtime, It is forming alias name while aggregating for Y-axis. 

Graph is not displaying because of this alias name which is created on runtime. Can you please let me know of how it is handled in your application?

entry.title: Re: use TREX for more SAP systems?
entry.content: Can you please share the steps to add multiple SAP systems(DEV, QAS, Sandbox) to single TREX system

entry.title: Re: Adding 2 different embedded connectors in same TREX instance
entry.content: Hi Could you please share the detailed steps for connecting DEV & Sandbox to Single TREX

Did you get the solution?? facing the same issue

entry.title: leave not due (Half Pay) configuration
entry.content:
i want to add absence type "leave not due (Half Pay)" in absence types such that it cannot be used unless quota is available, when i try to asssign this absence type to user , it shows an error message (i.e.,Earned Leave Quota Exists. 'Leave Not Due' are not allowed to avail). it can only used when qouta is not available, how i can configure this in my system step by step ?

I attached S.S of Leave not due with full pay , but i want to create with Half pay!

entry.title: Re: CIT (Corporate Income Tax) solution in Poland
entry.content: Dear Agnes, has there been any update since 2021?

entry.title: Re: Manage Supplier Quotations Custom Field/Logic
entry.content: I updated the question section and added custom logic and errors screenshot in the application.
